travel, traveling
I usually travel in September.
My husband always talks to the receptionist.
Right now I am traveling in July.
Today I am talking to him, and my husband is sitting down.
I am looking forward to walking around Lisbon.
Good morning, and welcome to the Hotel Lisboa. Are you checking in?
Good morning. Yes, we are. We have a reservation, under the name Oliveira.
Let me see... I am sorry. I am looking at the computer, and I am not finding anything under Oliveira.
Could you look under Pinheiro de Oliveira, please? It is a long name. My full name is on my passport.
Ah, here it is. Pinheiro de Oliveira. Two people, six nights. Is that right?
Yes, that is right. We are staying for six nights, and we are leaving on the twelfth.
Perfect. Room four eleven, on the fourth floor. Here is your room key. The elevator is on your left, next to the lobby.
Thank you. What time is breakfast? And I am sorry, could you repeat the room number, please?
Of course. Room four eleven. Breakfast is from seven to ten, here on the ground floor. Are you looking forward to Lisbon?
I am! I am looking forward to walking around the old city. My husband is parking the car right now. He is not looking forward to that!
Good morning. We have a reservation under the name Pinheiro de Oliveira.
We are staying for six nights.
Excuse me, the overhead bin is full. Where can I put my carry-on?
Could you repeat the room number, please?

Good afternoon, ladies and gentlemen. This is an announcement for the passengers of flight two nineteen to Lisbon. We are now boarding at gate twenty-two. We are starting with rows twenty to thirty, and after that we are calling all the other rows. Please have your passport and your boarding pass ready in your hand. When you are walking into the plane, a flight attendant is standing at the door, and she is helping you find your seat. Your carry-on goes in the overhead bin, above your seat, and your small bag goes under the seat in front of you. Please do not stand in the aisle, because other passengers are walking behind you. When everybody is sitting down, we are closing the door, so please close your seat belt and listen to the safety instructions. We are flying to Lisbon this afternoon, and the flight is nine hours and thirty minutes. We are landing in Lisbon tomorrow morning, at eight o'clock, local time. Thank you very much, and welcome on board.

Hello. My name is Sofia, and I am sitting here in the hotel lobby, like you. What a long day! Right now I am waiting for my husband. He is not here, because he is parking the car, and he is taking a long time: the street is very small and there are cars everywhere. And the receptionist? He is looking for our reservation on the computer right now. It is under my husband's name, not mine, and that is always a problem. You know, I usually travel with my sister, in September. But this year is different: right now I am traveling with my husband, in July, and I am looking forward to every day of it. Do you know what I am looking forward to the most? A hot shower and a big bed. We are staying here for six nights, and tomorrow we are visiting the old city. Oh, the receptionist is calling me now. He has our room key, and I think we are on the fourth floor. The elevator is over there, on the right. It was very nice to talk to you. Enjoy your stay!
